MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-20-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Continued To Aug-26-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Brian A. Angelini, esq. (310-507-7900 x.105) of Bedford Law Group for Plaintiff Boylan via court call.
David A. Delbon, esq. (415-554-3962) Deputy City Attorney of San Francisco for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

The Court announces its tentative ruling.
RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied. On the cause of action for dangerous condition of public property pursuant to Government Code 835, there is exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the City had actual and/or constructive notice of the bolts prior to the accident. (Peterson Dec., 3-5; Mathias Depo., 12:16-21; 13:22-14:5.) On summary judgment, "[t]he evidence of the moving party should be strictly construed, and that of the opponent liberally construed, and any doubts as to the propriety of granting the motion should be resolved in favor of the party opposing the motion." (Branco v. Kearny Moto Park, Inc. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 184, 189.) As to the cause of action for general negligence, the City correctly argues that its liability is controlled by statute pursuant to Government Code 815(a) and there is no common law claim for negligence against it. (See, e.g., Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 829 ["a public entity is not liable for injuries except as provided by statute ( 815) and . . . section 835 sets out the exclusive conditions under which a public entity is liable for injuries caused by a dangerous condition of public property."].) The Court, however, cannot grant summary adjudication on that cause of action because the City failed to seek summary adjudication in the alternative. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript).

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
